story.text: As bdo, I want to stamp the slow loop's bounds once and then let in-fence dial proposals admit themselves, while any out-of-fence proposal still escalates to me — because the slow loop senses the field and proposes a setpoint change every tick, and routing each proposal to my queue floods me, but a blanket auto-admit hands the dial away. The fence is the arc-confirm shape applied to the dial: one standing stamp of the safe range, the loop self-admits within it, and a single breached key escalates the whole proposal.

briefing.headline: The slow loop's bounded auto-admit fence: in-fence dial proposals self-admit
briefing.value: An admitted auto_admit_fence record (signed --by bdo) declares the safe range for each setpoint key. The disposer then folds each slow-loop dial proposal against the fence: every key in-bounds -> the proposal self-admits as a setpoint change; any key out-of-bounds -> the whole proposal escalates to bdo (one breached key fails the lot). The owner stamps the bounds once instead of judging every tick, and never loses the dial: cooling is always allowed, out-of-fence always escalates.
briefing.why_now: The slow loop proposes a dial change every tick (e.g. step_budget 3->4) and the disposition seam was answered (bounded auto-admit) but unbuilt; without the fence each proposal either floods bdo's queue or is ignored. This is the propose-vs-dispose resolution made executable.
briefing.if_accepted: The field's outcomes move their own dial within bdo's stamped bounds, with receipts; out-of-bounds proposals still arrive at his queue. The §10 teeth: a proposal with one in-fence key and one out-of-fence key is each locally fine yet refuses to fit one auto-admit — the disposer escalates the whole thing rather than admitting the safe half.
briefing.if_rejected: The slow loop keeps proposing into a void or into bdo's queue; the system cannot regulate its own pace without either flooding the owner or handing him the dial entirely.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: the fence is an admitted record (superseded, never erased); with no fence admitted, nothing auto-admits (the loop still proposes, bdo still disposes). Activation is bdo's stamp (--by bdo); the mechanism is inert until he sets the bounds.
briefing.mechanism: loop/disposer.py folds proposals against the admitted auto_admit_fence; loop/orchestrate.py wires it into the tick; tests/test_disposer.py carries the §10 teeth (one breached key escalates the whole proposal; cooling always allowed; no fence = no auto-admit). Done-line 0091.

_note: Birth record for the slow loop's bounded auto-admit fence (done-line 0091), authored so the off-log work carries an atom on the log under the now-live atom-invariant gate. Never mutated; verdicts are the fold over .ai-native/log/ (D-5). The fence itself is bdo's stamp (auto_admit_fence --by bdo); this atom is the mechanism, inert until he sets the bounds.
